author: schelv
text: NER predictions on long texts incorrect for models trained using BERT and transformers version 3

author: schelv
text:
**Describe the bug**  
For long texts only the NER predictions on the first part of the text make sense. (maybe it is the 512 token boundary?? (I didn't count =P)). This happens when the model is trained using transformers version 3. When using flair with transformers version 2 and loading a model trained with v. 2. the output is correct.

edit: apparently it happens for transformers version > 3.0.0

**To Reproduce**  
This behavior happens for NER models I trained with transformers v3. But it happens also when downloading a pretrained NER model (e.g. "nl-ner").

**Environment:**

* flair version does not seem to matter 0.5.1, 0.6.1 output is the same
* transformers 3.\*

author: schelv
text: Option for TransformerWordEmbeddings to process long sentences.

author: schelv
text: newer transformer versions use a different type of tokenizer. this solves the problem.
